AlphaGeo's Parag Khanna On The New World Order
data.description:
Best-selling author and futurist Parag Khanna says the new world order has shifted to a polynodal world -- where power is distributed among dynamic regional hubs like Singapore, Dubai, and Mumbai. He discusses his outlook with Shery Ahn and Avril Hong on the sidelines of the Bloomberg New Economy Forum 2025 in Singapore.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: Novo Nordisk Alzheimer's studies could yield answers on GLP-1 benefit
data.description:
STORY: Studies from Novo Nordisk due early next month will give the strongest indication yet of whether blockbuster GLP-1 drugs - a class being used by millions for diabetes and weight loss - can slow progress of Alzheimer's disease.

Two trials have been described by Novo as a "lottery ticket".

They're testing its older diabetes pill that has the same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy in thousands of people with mild Alzheimer's.

The goal is to decrease patients' rate of cognitive decline by at least 20%.

Alzheimer's experts say success would usher in a convenient new treatment to slow progression in a condition that affects 50 million people globally.

Researchers expect to glean important clues for future study into how and why these types of drugs, which include Eli Lilly's Zepbound and Mounjaro, may be useful against the disease.

There are currently two drugs approved for slowing Alzheimer's by removing amyloid from the brain.

They are Lilly's Kisunla,  Leqembi from Eisai, and Biogen.

They were shown in clinical trials to slow disease progression by close to 30%.

Although they also carry the risk of serious side effects and require regular scans.

Novo is slated to present its results on December 3rd, although it could announce some data before then.

data.title: BNP Paribas Announces Management Changes
data.description:
BNP Paribas announced the appointment of a custody boss. Former JP Morgan head of product for custody and fund services, David Braga, is to take on the new role in the middle of May, 2026. Braga is also chair of the Australian Custodial Services Association.

He takes over from acting head of BNP Paribas Securities Services, Ian Perkins.

data.title: South Africa is discussing possible G20 participation with US, president says
data.description:
JOHANNESBURG (Reuters) -South Africa is discussing with United States the possibility of participation in the G20 summit after an initial boycott, President Cyril Ramaphosa said on Thursday.

Ramaphosa was speaking at joint news conference with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and EU Council President António Costa.

"It comes at the eleventh hour," he said, adding that they would have to see what's possible in terms of how U.S. officials could participate.

(Reporting by Tim Cocks; Editing by Toby Chopra)

data.title: Google Launches Nano Banana Pro, Promising Better Reasoning, Text Generation
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall


Google released Nano Banana Pro, an updated version of its artificial-intelligence image editing and generation platform built on Gemini 3 Pro.

The company said Thursday that the new tool will generate more accurate and legible text within AI images. The new tool will take advantage of Gemini 3's reasoning capabilities and feature improved consistency and creative controls, Google said.

Google said free-tier Gemini users will receive limited free quotas to use Nano Banana Pro. The tool also is being rolled out into Google Search's AI Mode, Gemini API, Google Ads, and Flow, Google's AI filmmaking tool.

The launch comes two days after Google launched Gemini 3 in a bid to close its gap with OpenAI and other artificial intelligence companies, The Wall Street Journal reported Tuesday.

Though ChatGPT use still outpaces Gemini, The Journal reported that the August launch of Nano Banana, one of the fastest image generators on the market, has accelerated growth and user retention.


Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-20-25 1058ET

data.title: Mega Financial Holding Co., Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Mega Financial Holding Co., Ltd. reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net interest income was TWD 9,306.48 million compared to TWD 9,014.14 million a year ago. Net income was TWD 10,094.84 million compared to TWD 8,924.09 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 0.68 compared to TWD 0.6 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 0.68 compared to TWD 0.6 a year ago.
For the nine months, net interest income was TWD 28,280.43 million compared to TWD 27,654.08 million a year ago. Net income was TWD 28,285.4 million compared to TWD 29,320.77 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 1.91 compared to TWD 1.98 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 1.91 compared to TWD 1.98 a year ago.
